structure.description: Below the resolved-backlinks rail, a secondary 'unlinked mentions' section lists entities that mention the current one by raw title (not by formal link), each with a one-click 'linkify' affordance. Promotes implicit references to explicit edges, lets the graph self-densify by user assent rather than auto-magic.

rationale.hypothesis: In a vault that grew organically, many connections were typed before the target existed (or by an author who didn't bother with brackets). A separate channel for 'I think this is also a reference, please confirm' lets the system expose latent edges without committing them — user remains the arbiter, but the cost of confirmation is one click.

evidence.description: Backlinks panel has 'Unlinked mentions' subsection with linkify button per match

counterexample.description: Mentions are explicit only; no fuzzy-match suggestions — different model

shouldMatch.reason: Block.content full-text match against Page.title can produce unlinked-mention candidates

shouldNotMatch.reason: References are formal/auditable, fuzzy linkify would violate evidence integrity
Mentions are scoped to participants, no fuzzy resolution makes sense
